---
title: "Clinical genomics evidence application"
engine: pi-bio
format: gfm
---

This is an executable downstream application of `pi-bio-agent`, not a proposed core workflow. It composes the
workbench's declared relations into two traversal lanes over one case:

- **direct** starts from observed variants and retains candidates, abstentions, and evidence conflicts;
- **inverted** grounds the case narrative, walks phenotype/disease/gene relations, resolves assembly-pinned
  intervals, reads only those indexed VCF regions, and annotates selected alleles.

Both lanes materialize into `case_evidence`. The application owns phenotype policy, coverage semantics, ranking,
review items, and the evidence packet. Core supplies manifest execution, DuckDB materialization, bounded HTTP
fanout, checkpoints, CAS, replay, and observations.

## Hermetic host composition

The executable document uses recorded grounding, a local projection of the Monarch relation contract, an indexed
VCF test case, and a local VEP-compatible HTTP server. These inputs make the woven proof deterministic; they are not
the browser product's case or evidence source. The server deliberately returns two `503` responses before
succeeding, exercising the generic DuckNNG fanout/retry path without relying on a live endpoint.

```{ts .pi-bio}
import assert from "node:assert/strict";
import { createServer } from "node:http";
import { promises as fs } from "node:fs";
import { tmpdir } from "node:os";
import { dirname, join, relative, resolve, sep } from "node:path";
import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url";
import {
  getClinicalReviewQueue,
  listClinicalAnalyses,
  listClinicalReanalysisQueue,
  runClinicalGenomicsWorkbench,
  updateClinicalReviewDisposition,
} from "../../dist/clinical-genomics.js";
import { registerClinicalCaseRevision } from "../../dist/clinical-case-registry.js";
import { localCandidateVariantSearchRuntime } from "../../dist/candidate-variant-search.js";
import { localMonarchFixtureRuntime } from "../../dist/monarch-host.js";
import { loadRecordedGroundingRuntime } from "../../dist/recorded-grounding.js";

const sourceDir = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
const workspace = await fs.mkdtemp(join(tmpdir(), "pi-bio-clinical-application-"));
await fs.cp(sourceDir, workspace, {
  recursive: true,
  filter: (source) => relative(sourceDir, source).split(sep)[0] !== ".pi",
});

const annotations = {
  "17-43093464-A-T": { geneId: "HGNC:GENEB", gene: "GENEB", transcriptId: "ENST_FIXTURE_GENEB", consequence: "stop_gained", impact: "HIGH", af: 0.0002, significance: "pathogenic" },
  "17-43093470-C-G": { geneId: "HGNC:GENEB", gene: "GENEB", transcriptId: "ENST_FIXTURE_GENEB", consequence: "missense_variant", impact: "MODERATE", af: 0.0003, significance: "uncertain_significance" },
  "17-43093470-C-T": { geneId: "HGNC:GENEB", gene: "GENEB", transcriptId: "ENST_FIXTURE_GENEB", consequence: "stop_gained", impact: "HIGH", af: 0.02, significance: "benign" },
};

let requests = 0;
const server = createServer(async (request, response) => {
  const chunks = [];
  for await (const chunk of request) chunks.push(Buffer.isBuffer(chunk) ? chunk : Buffer.from(chunk));
  requests += 1;
  if (request.method !== "POST" || request.url !== "/vep") {
    response.writeHead(404).end();
    return;
  }
  if (requests <= 2) {
    response.writeHead(503, { "content-type": "application/json" });
    response.end(JSON.stringify({ error: "transient_fixture_failure" }));
    return;
  }
  const variants = JSON.parse(Buffer.concat(chunks).toString("utf8")).variants;
  const rows = variants.map((input) => {
    const [chrom, pos, _dot, ref, alt] = input.split(" ");
    const key = `${chrom}-${pos}-${ref}-${alt}`;
    const item = annotations[key];
    return {
      input,
      most_severe_consequence: item.consequence,
      transcript_consequences: [{
        gene_id: item.geneId,
        gene_symbol: item.gene,
        transcript_id: item.transcriptId,
        biotype: "protein_coding",
        canonical: 1,
        impact: item.impact,
        consequence_terms: [item.consequence],
      }],
      colocated_variants: [{ id: key, clin_sig: [item.significance], frequencies: { [alt]: { gnomadg: item.af } } }],
    };
  });
  response.writeHead(200, { "content-type": "application/json" });
  response.end(JSON.stringify(rows));
});
await new Promise((resolveListen, reject) => {
  server.once("error", reject);
  server.listen(0, "127.0.0.1", resolveListen);
});
const address = server.address();
assert.ok(address && typeof address !== "string");

const runtime = {
  url: `http://127.0.0.1:${address.port}/vep`,
  headersJson: '[{"name":"Content-Type","value":"application/json"},{"name":"Accept","value":"application/json"}]',
  sourceId: "fixture:vep",
  sourceVersion: "fixture-1",
  duckdbInitSql: ["LOAD ducknng"],
};

const narrative = "The proband was late to sit and walk and has hypotonia. Her mother has no seizures. Possible ataxia remains uncertain.";
const caseRevision = await registerClinicalCaseRevision(workspace, {
  caseId: "CASE-RD-001",
  revisionId: "application-r1",
  indexMemberIds: ["proband"],
  members: [
    { memberId: "proband", role: "proband", affectedStatus: "affected", sex: "female" },
    { memberId: "mother", role: "mother", affectedStatus: "unaffected", sex: "female" },
  ],
  relationships: [
    { fromMemberId: "mother", predicate: "parent_of", toMemberId: "proband" },
  ],
  assets: [
    {
      assetId: "narrative",
      kind: "clinical_narrative",
      mediaType: "text/plain",
      bytes: Buffer.from(narrative),
      memberIds: ["proband", "mother"],
    },
    {
      assetId: "variants",
      kind: "variant_set",
      mediaType: "application/gzip",
      format: "vcf.gz",
      assembly: "GRCh38",
      bytes: await fs.readFile(join(workspace, "data", "case_variants.vcf.gz")),
      memberIds: ["proband"],
    },
    {
      assetId: "variants-index",
      kind: "variant_index",
      mediaType: "application/octet-stream",
      format: "tbi",
      bytes: await fs.readFile(join(workspace, "data", "case_variants.vcf.gz.tbi")),
      memberIds: ["proband"],
      attributes: { index_for: "variants" },
    },
    {
      assetId: "variant-table",
      kind: "variant_table",
      mediaType: "text/csv",
      format: "csv",
      bytes: await fs.readFile(join(workspace, "data", "case_variants.csv")),
      memberIds: ["proband"],
    },
  ],
  recordedAt: "2026-07-12T11:55:00Z",
});

const run = async (now) => runClinicalGenomicsWorkbench({
  exampleDir: workspace,
  caseId: "CASE-RD-001",
  caseRevisionId: caseRevision.revisionId,
  analysisId: "application-proof",
  now,
  grounding: await loadRecordedGroundingRuntime(join(workspace, "data", "grounding_proposals.json")),
  hypotheses: localMonarchFixtureRuntime(workspace),
  variantSearch: localCandidateVariantSearchRuntime(workspace),
  vep: runtime,
});
```

## Execute and resume

```{ts .pi-bio}
let first;
let resumed;
try {
  first = await run("2026-07-12T12:00:00Z");
  resumed = await run("2026-07-12T12:05:00Z");
} finally {
  await new Promise((resolveClose, reject) => server.close((error) => error ? reject(error) : resolveClose()));
}

assert.equal(first.workflow.executedSteps, 9);
assert.equal(first.workflow.reusedSteps, 0);
assert.equal(resumed.workflow.executedSteps, 0);
assert.equal(resumed.workflow.reusedSteps, 9);
assert.equal(resumed.packetDigest, first.packetDigest);
assert.equal(requests, 3, "resume must reuse the VEP checkpoint");
assert.equal(first.packet.inputRevision?.revisionId, caseRevision.revisionId);

assert.equal(first.packet.summary.directCandidates, 1);
assert.equal(first.packet.summary.directAbstentions, 1);
assert.equal(first.packet.summary.resolvedCandidateGenes, 2);
assert.equal(first.packet.summary.selectedAlleles, 3);
assert.equal(first.packet.summary.invertedSupportedHypotheses, 1);
assert.equal(first.packet.summary.invertedGaps, 1);
assert.equal(first.packet.summary.conflicts, 2);

const direct = first.packet.lanes.direct.rows;
assert.equal(direct.find((row) => row.variant_key === "2-47637258-C-CT")?.variant_bucket, "abstain_no_frequency");
const inverted = first.packet.lanes.inverted.rows;
assert.equal(
  inverted.find((row) => row.gene === "GENEB" && row.evidence_status === "genotype_supports_hypothesis")?.annotation_consequence,
  "stop_gained",
);

const initialReviews = await getClinicalReviewQueue(workspace, first.analysisId);
assert.equal(initialReviews.found, true);
if (!initialReviews.found) throw new Error("expected a recorded review queue");
const frequencyReview = initialReviews.reviews.find((item) => item.kind === "resolve_frequency");
assert.ok(frequencyReview);
await updateClinicalReviewDisposition(workspace, first.analysisId, {
  reviewId: frequencyReview.reviewId,
  status: "needs_follow_up",
  note: "Obtain a declared frequency source.",
  now: "2026-07-12T12:10:00Z",
});

const history = await listClinicalAnalyses(workspace, { caseId: first.packet.caseId });
assert.deepEqual(history.map((item) => item.analysisId), [first.analysisId]);
const latestCases = await listClinicalReanalysisQueue(workspace);
assert.equal(latestCases.length, 1);
assert.equal(latestCases[0]?.analysisId, first.analysisId);
assert.equal(latestCases[0]?.state, "needs_follow_up");
assert.equal(latestCases[0]?.needsFollowUpItems, 1);

piBio.json({
  application: "clinical-genomics",
  workflow: {
    first: { executedSteps: first.workflow.executedSteps, reusedSteps: first.workflow.reusedSteps },
    resumed: { executedSteps: resumed.workflow.executedSteps, reusedSteps: resumed.workflow.reusedSteps },
    replayDigestStableWithinAnalysis: resumed.workflow.replayDigest === first.workflow.replayDigest,
    packetDigestStable: resumed.packetDigest === first.packetDigest,
    vepRequestsIncludingRetries: requests,
  },
  evidence: first.packet.summary,
  provenance: {
    runCount: first.packet.provenance.runIds.length,
    packetStoredInCas: first.packetUri.startsWith("cas:sha256:"),
  },
  reviewProjection: {
    recordedAnalysesForCase: history.length,
    latestCaseState: latestCases[0]?.state,
    needsFollowUpItems: latestCases[0]?.needsFollowUpItems,
  },
});
```

## Review state and case reanalysis projection

The evidence packet is immutable after it is committed. A reviewer records a disposition as a separate temporal
ledger observation keyed to one packet review item; that disposition does not revise a variant assessment or move to
a later analysis automatically. The browser's reanalysis pane projects the latest recorded analysis for each case and
orders explicit states such as follow-up, reanalysis signal, conflict, evidence gap, and open review. It is a
transparent work queue, not a diagnostic ranking or clinical classification.

## What the application establishes

The run establishes that the public substrate can support an application with an immutable registered case revision,
grounded inputs, foreign-graph queries, indexed range reads, bounded network retry, SQL reconciliation, nine durable
checkpoints, a CAS-backed packet, and exact resume. It also preserves an important abstention: missing population
frequency is not evidence that a variant is rare.

It does not establish ACMG/AMP classification, diagnosis, clinical validity, or live-source stability. Those are
application evaluation and review concerns.

## Source-pinned operation authoring

The browser's Variants pane starts from registered published rows and keeps the workbook observation separate from
an independently resolved NCBI variant identity and current ClinVar observation. The Compositions pane turns one
selected, resolved row into an operation-authoring slot. It accepts an operation identity, read-only SQL, a fixture,
and expected rows; admits only deterministic reads over the server-provided `variant_context`; executes the fixture
in an isolated DuckDB with external access disabled; checks the physical plan; then runs the operation through the
normal manifest runner over the source-pinned row. The run, receipts, replay, result, and candidate document are
stored in the ledger and CAS before the exact content digest can receive a terminal human decision. Approval records
a temporal skill revision; rejection cannot be undone by resubmitting the same bytes.

This is application composition over the public substrate, not a second execution system. The implementation is
[`composition-addon.ts`](../../src/composition-addon.ts), the browser interaction is
[`compositions.js`](../../web/addons/compositions.js), and
[`composition-addon.test.ts`](../../test/composition-addon.test.ts) proves relation admission, deterministic-only
execution, fixture failure, real source-bound execution, CAS and graph linkage, exact-digest approval, rejection,
and immutable terminal decisions. The Playwright suite exercises the park-review-activate interaction at desktop
width and its stacked mobile layout.

## Evidence source plane

Case workup uses a retrieval hierarchy rather than a general literature corpus:

1. Query registered case, family, phenotype, variant, prior-assessment, and previously extracted evidence relations
   with SQL.
2. Retrieve a publication only when the required evidence has not been structured already.
3. Extract source-spanned claims once and register the text span, normalized entities, source identity, observation
   time, and extraction decision in DuckDB, the graph, and CAS.
4. Reuse those claims across cases and later reanalysis while preserving their original source and observation time.
5. Run deterministic ACMG/AMP or CNV/SV policy over typed evidence relations.
6. Invoke an agent only for ambiguous extraction, evidence conflict, or an expert-facing proposal; the model does not
   become the fact source or the final clinical decision.

Case notes, uploaded PDFs, and PDF/DOCX/XLSX supplements use the same evidence plane, including literature available
only through a user's or institution's lawful access. The host registers the original bytes and access policy in CAS;
a declared `compute.run` parser emits text, document structure, tables, figures, and page coordinates as named outputs;
SQL selects bounded source windows; and an agent may propose typed claims. A claim is admissible only when it carries
the source digest and an exact text span or table/figure region that a deterministic validator can resolve against the
pinned parse. Approval creates an immutable evidence revision, and derived parse/extraction artifacts inherit the
source's access restrictions. The application does not bypass a paywall or send licensed full text across the host
trust boundary unless the host policy explicitly permits it.

Parsing is a host-selected profile rather than a fixed model dependency. Prefer structured JATS/XML or HTML from PMC,
Europe PMC, or an authorized publisher source when available; PubMed normally supplies publication identity, metadata,
abstracts, and links rather than the full article. For uploaded PDFs, use native text and layout extraction first, OCR
for scanned pages, and a multimodal provider for figures or unresolved layouts when policy permits. Every parse records
the provider and model/version, input digest, page/region coordinates, confidence, and run receipts. OCR and vision
outputs are derived observations: a promoted claim still cites the original document digest and resolvable source
region, while low-confidence or disagreeing parses become review or abstention states.

The useful implemented pattern in
[`cur-ai-ss`'s typed evidence blocks](https://github.com/clingen-data-model/cur-ai-ss/blob/ca25342a83806ce4674303ed39cd2f7c54050a52/lib/models/evidence_block.py)
and [document parser](https://github.com/clingen-data-model/cur-ai-ss/blob/ca25342a83806ce4674303ed39cd2f7c54050a52/lib/misc/pdf/parse.py)
is field-level evidence with quotes and table/figure locators over parsed papers and supplements. The local contract is
stricter: source bytes and parses are content-addressed, locators are verified, extraction actor and contract digests
are recorded, and `false`, `unknown`, `not captured`, and `not observed in a declared search scope` remain distinct.
The external application's database, worker lifecycle, source-specific annotators, and model-mediated segregation
counting are not imported.

Provider choice follows the same separation. LitVar2 is a variant-to-publication index; Europe PMC and PMC are the
preferred metadata, full-text, and annotation sources when license and access permit. OpenAlex contributes scholarly
identity, citation, institution, and regional graph context, not variant evidence. Licensed discovery or reranking
services may be host-injected, and broad web search remains a last-resort locator. Search results and generated
summaries are never accepted as evidence without a registered primary source and source-spanned claim.

The [MARRVEL 2 codebase](https://github.com/LiuzLab/MARRVEL2) makes useful operation families concrete: variant
normalization and liftover; variant-to-gene mapping; population, clinical-assertion, patient-cohort, structural-overlap,
and predictor lookups; and gene disease, constraint, orthology, model-organism phenotype, expression, ontology,
interaction, and protein-domain projections. These are requirements for composable source operations, not a reason
to mirror MARRVEL's database or preserve every endpoint as an isolated skill. Each adapter must retain the upstream
authority, version, license boundary, missingness, and receipt; SQL then reduces those relations for the current
case. Population and curated clinical assertions may enter deterministic classification only through criterion-specific
contracts. Orthology, interaction, domain, expression, and model-organism signals remain research prioritization
unless an approved guideline explicitly admits them.

The [Monarch Initiative 2024](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38000386/) platform already integrates open phenotype,
gene, disease, ontology, and cross-species knowledge as a semantic graph with APIs and analytic services. This
repository already closes over that graph: [`monarch.manifest.json`](monarch.manifest.json) attaches the release-pinned
DuckDB graph and its declared operation joins canonical nodes and edges through ontology closure; the
[`monarch-inverted.test.ts`](../../test/monarch-inverted.test.ts) proof executes that operation through the normal run,
receipt, replay, and CAS path. `npm run pattern:monarch --workspace=packages/workbench` is the live application entry
point. Monarch is therefore the implemented graph plane where it overlaps MARRVEL, not a future provider gap.
MARRVEL remains valuable as an operation inventory, an optional aggregating provider, and a comparison surface for
sources absent from Monarch. The durable product surface is the tested composition and its evidence lineage, not a
hand-maintained catalog of wrappers.

## Functional evidence calibration

[MaveDB](https://www.mavedb.org/docs/mavedb/programmatic-access/api-quickstart.html) is the authoritative source for
multiplexed assay score sets. A calibration experiment imports the score-set document, score CSV, mapped GA4GH VRS
variants, calibration revisions and assignments, release-selected ClinVar controls, and gnomAD controls as separate
relations. Every response is CAS-pinned with its URL, content digest, observation time, and available freshness token.
The score-set URN does not identify one timeless interpretation: multiple investigator and community calibrations may
coexist, and primary, research-use-only, evidence-strength, source-publication, and publication-state fields belong to
the calibration revision.

The JAG1 membrane-expression score set `urn:mavedb:00001198-a-1` is a concrete target. MaveDB exposes 2,832 real
variant measurements, replicate scores and counts, multiple historical ClinVar control snapshots, gnomAD v4.1
controls, and direct variant-to-calibration assignments. Its likelihood-ratio calibration cites
[Hayeck et al.](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ajhg.2026.06.016) and assigns PS3 at supporting, moderate, or strong strength
for selected abnormal classes. Its normal classes deliberately carry no benign ACMG evidence because membrane
expression does not test every relevant JAG1 function. Deterministic ACMG policy must consume that explicit evidence
assignment; it must not infer BS3 merely from a normal assay score.

The agent can run new calibration experiments, but the experiment is declared compute rather than an unrecorded
Python session:

1. SQL selects one pinned score-set revision, one predeclared ClinVar snapshot, gnomAD controls, synonymous controls,
   and an explicit train/validation split. Variants used to create or reinterpret the assay calibration are excluded
   from held-out evaluation, and overlapping labels are resolved by a recorded rule rather than random choice.
2. `compute.run` invokes a commit- or image-pinned calibration implementation with an explicit random seed and
   observed environment. It emits model parameters, per-score likelihood ratios or direct class assignments,
   uncertainty intervals, diagnostics, and declared plot files.
3. CAS captures the input relation, code and environment identities, split, fitted model, metrics, and figures. SQL
   compares the candidate against threshold and likelihood baselines using held-out log loss, Brier score,
   calibration, coverage, likelihood-ratio confidence intervals, and control violations.
4. The candidate is parked through the same validation and exact-digest review lifecycle as Composition Studio. Only
   an approved, non-RUO revision may emit a criterion-specific PS3/BS3 evidence relation; neither the model nor the
   calibration experiment emits a final variant classification.

This is already supported by the substrate's host-injected `compute.run`, environment attestation, output
declarations, ledger, CAS, and temporal skill revision contracts. The missing application work is a validated
calibration profile and evaluation protocol for each assay family, not another core runtime.

FAVOR has two distinct roles. The [FAVOR v2 beta API](https://api-v2.genohub.org/docs) provides compact or detailed
variant lookup, 10,000-variant batch lookup, graph operations, and a convenient MaveDB read model; it is useful for
interactive annotation and source discovery. Its current variant-band response, however, does not expose calibration
URN, primary status, or research-use-only status. It cannot be the sole clinical evidence contract until those source
identities are retained and checked against authoritative MaveDB records. The
[FAVORannotator](https://favor.genohub.org/favor-annotator) R/PostgreSQL path is the bulk WGS/WES offer: run it as a
declared host compute profile, or attach release-pinned bulk tables and join in DuckDB, rather than issuing one HTTP
request per cohort variant. FAVOR predictors and aggregate scores remain annotation features unless a guideline-owned
criterion contract explicitly admits them; they are not interchangeable with empirical MAVE functional evidence.

## Evaluation contract

The product unit is an immutable case revision, not a paper-specific workbook row. Each revision binds a proband,
family and pedigree structure when available, one or more source-description revisions, registered variant-call or
annotation assets for the relevant family members, and zero or one prior assessment. A missing prior assessment is a
valid first-analysis state. New, previous, and additional descriptions remain separate source revisions so every HPO
assertion can retain its exact source span and later reanalysis can distinguish changed evidence from changed logic.

Three evaluation levels must remain separate:

1. **End-to-end case prioritization.** A host-supplied case/truth bundle measures causal gene and allele recall at
   configurable `k`, precision at `k`, reciprocal rank, calibrated abstention, search coverage, evidence conflicts,
   reviewer workload, and classification concordance. Family and multiplex cases are evaluated against their declared
   member roles and segregation truth rather than flattened into a proband-only row. Confirmatory results and expert
   adjudications remain evaluator-only evidence until the host explicitly registers them.
2. **Criterion and component conformance.** Source-pinned real variants test each deterministic evaluator against an
   independently curated evidence record. Small synthetic vectors remain appropriate for null, boundary, cap,
   dependency, exclusivity, and malformed-input behavior, but they do not establish clinical performance. A
   variant-only classification workbook may contribute component regression rows; it cannot substitute for the
   case-level product evaluation or determine the application architecture.
3. **Temporal reanalysis.** Immutable case, source, profile, annotation-release, and literature-claim revisions make
   change attributable. Evaluation measures newly recovered causal alleles, rank movement, changed evidence lines,
   abstentions resolved, false-positive workload, and diagnostic yield while preserving the previous result rather
   than overwriting it.

The public package does not embed a private clinical cohort or claim clinical validity from its hermetic case proof.
A cohort adapter must register case and truth assets as host inputs, project bounded relations into DuckDB, run each
case through the same checkpoint graph, and write per-case predictions and aggregate metrics to ledger/CAS. This
keeps evaluation data replaceable without replacing the product flow.

### Release-pinned reclassification harness

The workbench also now has a temporal ClinVar source plane for retrospective reclassification work. Raw release bytes
and a declared normalizer are pinned in CAS; normalized assertions remain in DuckLake at an exact release snapshot;
and SQL derives the assertion-to-variation, condition, and gene graph when needed. The ledger holds the release
identity, snapshot anchor, artifact references, and runs rather than a duplicate of the release-scale relation.

A blinded task copies only the baseline release into an agent workspace, passes an HMAC target commitment keyed by
evaluator-only entropy plus a declared baseline candidate policy, and runs that policy as a recorded SQL operation.
The target release, commitment secret, and source-label delta remain evaluator-only, so task metadata cannot be
matched against candidate target metadata by simple enumeration. This is meaningful only when the host actually
enforces separate tool/filesystem boundaries; a prompt or receipt cannot make a process blind. It tests source-label
change, not diagnostic truth or clinical validity.

The agent response is also durable evidence, not loose chat JSON. Each ranked prediction or explicit abstention binds
to the candidate run's manifest, replay, result, and run-object digests. The evaluator copies that verified CAS object
across the host boundary and computes per-row source-label agreement plus coverage, change recall, and reciprocal rank
in declared SQL. Proposal bytes are protected run bindings, so replay records their digest rather than their content.

```{ts .pi-bio}
import { createHash as createTemporalHash } from "node:crypto";
import {
  buildClinVarTemporalIsolationReceipt,
  defaultClinVarDuckLakeConfig,
  listClinVarReleases,
  prepareClinVarTemporalTask,
  registerClinVarRelease,
  registerClinVarTemporalProposalSet,
  runClinVarTemporalCandidates,
  runClinVarTemporalProposalEvaluation,
} from "../../dist/clinvar-temporal.js";

const temporalRoot = await fs.mkdtemp(join(tmpdir(), "pi-bio-clinvar-temporal-application-"));
const temporalEvaluatorWorkspace = join(temporalRoot, "evaluator");
const temporalAgentWorkspace = join(temporalRoot, "agent");
const temporalEvaluatorLake = defaultClinVarDuckLakeConfig(temporalEvaluatorWorkspace);
const temporalAgentLake = defaultClinVarDuckLakeConfig(temporalAgentWorkspace);
const temporalDigest = (value) => `sha256:${createTemporalHash("sha256").update(value).digest("hex")}`;
const temporalParser = {
  id: "application.clinvar-normalizer",
  version: "1.0.0",
  implementationDigest: temporalDigest("application.clinvar-normalizer@1.0.0"),
};
const temporalAssertion = {
  assertionId: "VCV000111",
  temporalKey: "vcv:000111",
  recordScope: "variation_aggregate",
  variationId: "111",
  conditionId: "MONDO:0000111",
  reviewStatus: "criteria provided, single submitter",
};
const temporalBaseline = await registerClinVarRelease(temporalEvaluatorWorkspace, {
  lake: temporalEvaluatorLake,
  releaseId: "baseline-visible",
  releasedAt: "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z",
  rawSource: {
    uri: "https://example.invalid/clinvar/baseline.tsv.gz",
    format: "tsv",
    mediaType: "text/tab-separated-values",
    bytes: Buffer.from("baseline fixture"),
  },
  parser: temporalParser,
  assertions: [
    { ...temporalAssertion, clinicalSignificance: "Uncertain significance" },
    {
      ...temporalAssertion,
      assertionId: "VCV000112",
      temporalKey: "vcv:000112",
      variationId: "112",
      conditionId: "MONDO:0000112",
      clinicalSignificance: "Uncertain significance",
    },
  ],
  recordedAt: "2026-02-02T00:00:00Z",
});
const temporalTarget = await registerClinVarRelease(temporalEvaluatorWorkspace, {
  lake: temporalEvaluatorLake,
  releaseId: "target-hidden",
  releasedAt: "2026-02-01T00:00:00Z",
  rawSource: {
    uri: "https://example.invalid/clinvar/target.tsv.gz",
    format: "tsv",
    mediaType: "text/tab-separated-values",
    bytes: Buffer.from("hidden target fixture"),
  },
  parser: temporalParser,
  assertions: [
    { ...temporalAssertion, clinicalSignificance: "Likely pathogenic" },
    {
      ...temporalAssertion,
      assertionId: "VCV000112",
      temporalKey: "vcv:000112",
      variationId: "112",
      conditionId: "MONDO:0000112",
      clinicalSignificance: "Uncertain significance",
    },
  ],
  recordedAt: "2026-02-02T00:00:00Z",
});
const temporalIsolation = buildClinVarTemporalIsolationReceipt({
  mode: "host_enforced",
  agentBoundaryId: "application-agent-boundary",
  evaluatorBoundaryId: "application-evaluator-boundary",
  targetAccess: "evaluator_only",
});
const temporalTask = await prepareClinVarTemporalTask({
  evaluatorWorkspace: temporalEvaluatorWorkspace,
  agentWorkspace: temporalAgentWorkspace,
  evaluatorLake: temporalEvaluatorLake,
  agentLake: temporalAgentLake,
  baseline: temporalBaseline,
  target: temporalTarget,
  candidatePolicy: { baselineClinicalSignificances: ["Uncertain significance"] },
  targetCommitmentSecret: Buffer.alloc(32, 23),
  isolationReceipt: temporalIsolation,
  taskId: "application-temporal-task",
  recordedAt: "2026-02-02T00:01:00Z",
});
assert.deepEqual(
  (await listClinVarReleases(temporalAgentWorkspace, temporalAgentLake)).map((release) => release.releaseId),
  [temporalBaseline.releaseId],
);
assert.equal(JSON.stringify(temporalTask.task).includes(temporalTarget.releaseId), false);

const temporalCandidates = await runClinVarTemporalCandidates(
  temporalAgentWorkspace,
  temporalAgentLake,
  temporalTask.task.taskId,
  { runId: "application-temporal-candidates", now: "2026-02-02T00:02:00Z" },
);
const temporalProposals = await registerClinVarTemporalProposalSet({
  agentWorkspace: temporalAgentWorkspace,
  agentLake: temporalAgentLake,
  taskId: temporalTask.task.taskId,
  proposalSetId: "application-temporal-proposals",
  candidateRunId: temporalCandidates.runId,
  actor: {
    id: "recorded-application-agent",
    version: "1.0.0",
    provider: "fixture",
    model: "recorded-proposals",
    contractDigest: temporalDigest("recorded-application-agent-contract@1.0.0"),
  },
  proposals: [
    {
      temporalKey: "vcv:000111",
      priorityRank: 1,
      prediction: "classification",
      predictedClinicalSignificance: "Likely pathogenic",
      confidence: 0.75,
      rationale: "Recorded baseline-only proposal for the executable application fixture.",
    },
    {
      temporalKey: "vcv:000112",
      priorityRank: 2,
      prediction: "abstain",
      rationale: "The fixture actor leaves this source-label prediction unresolved.",
    },
  ],
  recordedAt: "2026-02-02T00:03:00Z",
});
const temporalScores = await runClinVarTemporalProposalEvaluation({
  evaluatorWorkspace: temporalEvaluatorWorkspace,
  evaluatorLake: temporalEvaluatorLake,
  agentWorkspace: temporalAgentWorkspace,
  agentLake: temporalAgentLake,
  proposalSetId: temporalProposals.proposalSet.proposalSetId,
  scoreRunId: "application-temporal-scores",
  metricsRunId: "application-temporal-metrics",
  now: "2026-02-02T00:04:00Z",
});
assert.deepEqual(temporalScores.scores.rows.map((row) => row.score_status), ["correct", "abstained"]);
assert.equal(temporalScores.metrics.rows[0].coverage, 0.5);
assert.equal(temporalScores.metrics.rows[0].prediction_accuracy, 1);
assert.equal(temporalScores.metrics.rows[0].classification_accuracy, 1);
assert.equal(temporalScores.metrics.rows[0].target_change_reciprocal_rank, 1);
const temporalProposalDigestRecorded = temporalProposals.proposalSet.proposalDigest.startsWith("sha256:");
await fs.rm(temporalRoot, { recursive: true, force: true });

piBio.json({
  task: {
    id: temporalTask.task.taskId,
    target: "opaque commitment in agent boundary",
    candidateRun: temporalCandidates.runId,
  },
  proposal: {
    id: temporalProposals.proposalSet.proposalSetId,
    contentDigestRecorded: temporalProposalDigestRecorded,
    actor: temporalProposals.proposalSet.actor,
  },
  evaluator: {
    scoreRun: temporalScores.scores.runId,
    metricsRun: temporalScores.metrics.runId,
    metrics: temporalScores.metrics.rows[0],
  },
});
```

## Next case-workup closure

The same inverted lane can drive one resumable case study from registered VCF/gVCF/BCF/TSV assets and immutable
description revisions: source-spanned HPO assertions, phenotype/disease/gene retrieval, assembly-pinned range
restriction, bounded annotation, deterministic SVCv4 evidence evaluation, phenotype reranking, literature evidence,
and a gated ranked-candidate result. Family files retain sample and relationship identity; multiplex observations are
relations over members rather than prose appended to the proband. “One study” means one checkpoint graph, not one
prompt or opaque function.

The source-pinned SVCv4 draft now provides the score graph, caps, evidence admission, fail-closed classification gate,
`POP_FRQ` evaluator, and capture audit for all five currently specified CLN workflows. It does not invent the
unpublished CSpec scoring algorithms: those methods remain explicitly unimplemented until their authoritative
contracts can be pinned and tested. VEP and offline annotation tables are evidence sources, not classifiers.
Population observations, inheritance and segregation, de novo status, functional assays, curated assertions,
phenotype fit, literature claims, and reviewer judgment remain separately sourced; missing inputs produce typed
abstentions rather than inferred criteria.

## How applications change core

This application is an abstraction pressure surface. The correct movement is:

1. keep domain policy in its SQL relations and host composition;
2. note repeated friction while adding another application or generic pattern;
3. identify the common policy-free motion;
4. promote only that primitive to core with tests;
5. return this application to the public API and remove the workaround.

Bounded DuckNNG HTTP fanout followed that path. Phenotype ranking, variant-search coverage, and clinical evidence
states have not repeated elsewhere and remain here.
